name: Campsite
description: The Scout spots a campsite ahead — occupied, recently abandoned, or bearing darker signs. Other travellers leave traces on the long roads of Middle-earth.

events.label: A camp littered with corpses — ambushed and killed within the past few days. Orcs, bandits, or something worse? The silence is terrible.
events.on_eye: The killers are still in the area; 2 shadow points at the sight.
events.notes: Investigation/shadow event. Burial or identification may matter later.

events.label: A camp of bandits — or perhaps lawmen — holding prisoners. Who is in the right? The fellowship must decide whether to intervene, and on which side.
events.on_eye: The situation is more complex than it first appears; a wrong choice has lasting consequence.
events.notes: Moral dilemma. Insight and Scrutinise inform the choice.

events.label: A camp occupied by hostiles — orcs, brigands, servants of Shadow. They have not yet seen the fellowship.
events.on_eye: The camp is larger than first glimpsed, with sentries the Scout missed.
events.notes: Stealth or combat choice. Bypassing is often wisest.

events.label: A camp of neutral travellers — traders, pilgrims, a surveying party. They are wary but not hostile; may offer fire and news.
events.notes: Social opportunity. Links to Lookout — Traveller for flavour.

events.label: An unoccupied camp — fire gone cold, packs gone, no sign of trouble. The travellers simply moved on, but left useful things behind.
events.notes: Minor resupply opportunity. The finding may be good or cursed at GM discretion.

events.label: A table set out — bread, fruit, a flask of clear water — laid in the Elvish manner for travellers. A gift, or a trap? The cloth is familiar.
events.on_eye: Not an Elvish gift at all — an illusion concealing something else.
events.notes: Blessing event. Genuine Elvish gifts restore Hope; false ones carry danger.
